findings.message: This zero-match check is impossible after the planned rename: `VerifyFinding::warning` still contains the substring `Finding::warning`, so `rg 'Finding::warning'` will keep matching the updated convention prose even when the old name is gone. Use a boundary-anchored match for the standalone `Finding` token instead.

This grep is also a substring search, so it will still match the intended replacement `ReviewerFinding` in `pub use` lines and never reach zero after a correct rename. Anchor `Finding` as a standalone identifier token instead of `.*Finding\b`.

The grep checks in `verification.md` still use `\bFinding::error`, `\bFinding::warning`, `\bFindingError\b`, and `\bFinding\b`; those expressions also match `VerifyFinding`, `ReviewerFindingError`, and `ReviewerFinding`, so the zero-match assertions for T001/T003 and T004/T005 will fail even after a correct rename.

The T001/T004/T005 acceptance criteria in `spec.json` cite `knowledge/adr/2026-04-14-0625-finding-taxonomy-cleanup.md Canonical Block N`, but that ADR has no `Canonical Block` sections; those blocks live in the planner document. As written, the source attributions are not verifiable and should point at the actual planner sections instead.

This planner still introduces `T008` as a separate final CI-gate step, but the approved ADR and the track artifacts (`metadata.json` / `spec.json` / `verification.md`) all define only `T001`-`T007` and make `T007` the final gate. The off-by-one task numbering leaves the implementation order inconsistent across the planning docs.

The T004 self-check uses `grep -rn 'review_v2::Finding\b\|FindingError\|NonEmptyFindings' libs/domain/src/review_v2/`, which will still match `ReviewerFindingError`. As written, this check cannot prove the old `FindingError` name is gone and will false-fail once the rename lands. Use a boundary-aware pattern or split the checks, as done in `verification.md`.

T005 is incomplete: `review_v2` infra still uses `Finding` in `libs/infrastructure/src/review_v2/persistence/review_store.rs` and `libs/infrastructure/src/review_v2/persistence/tests.rs`, but the task only enumerates `codex_reviewer.rs` and `codex_local.rs`. Renaming the domain type will leave these files broken and `cargo test -p infrastructure` will fail.

The T004 self-check `grep -rn 'FindingError[^a-zA-Z_]' libs/domain/src/review_v2/` is a false-positive trap: it still matches `ReviewerFindingError::EmptyMessage`, so the planned zero-match verification cannot pass after the rename. Use a left-boundary-aware pattern like the one already used in `verification.md`.

`plan.md` understates T005: the rendered plan only names `libs/infrastructure/src/review_v2/codex_reviewer.rs` and `apps/cli/src/commands/review/codex_local.rs`, but the authoritative task/spec also require `libs/infrastructure/src/review_v2/persistence/review_store.rs` and `libs/infrastructure/src/review_v2/persistence/tests.rs`. That omission can cause an implementer to miss part of the review_v2 infrastructure rename.

T005 omits `libs/usecase/src/review_v2/tests.rs`, which imports `domain::review_v2::Finding` and constructs it with `Finding::new(...)`. As written, the rename plan leaves a usecase test file broken, and the listed verification (`cargo test -p infrastructure -p cli`) would not catch it.

The ADR rename table for `VerifyFinding` is incomplete: the planner and track docs explicitly treat `libs/domain/src/verify.rs`'s `/// A single verification finding.` comment as a load-bearing rename target, but D3 only enumerates the type/method/signature changes and omits that doc-comment update. That leaves the ADR cascade table out of sync with the plan/spec and makes the T001 scope easier to under-implement if someone follows the ADR alone.

T005 is under-specified: this ADR only names `libs/infrastructure/src/review_v2/codex_reviewer.rs` and `apps/cli/src/commands/review/codex_local.rs`, but the track plan/spec also require renaming `libs/infrastructure/src/review_v2/persistence/review_store.rs`, `libs/infrastructure/src/review_v2/persistence/tests.rs`, and `libs/usecase/src/review_v2/tests.rs`. Those files still contain live `Finding` references, so someone following the ADR alone would miss part of the rename cascade.

The verify-side cascade table stops at `VerifyOutcome::from_findings` / `findings()` / `add()` / `merge()` and omits `VerifyOutcome::pass`, `is_ok`, `has_errors`, and `error_count`, even though the planner/track metadata list those methods as part of the required `verify.rs` update set. That leaves the ADR incomplete and risks an implementation that misses those `VerifyOutcome` updates.

T006's precondition only mentions T004/T005, but the catalogue update also depends on the T001 `VerifyFinding` rename. As written, section S3 can be run before the verify-side domain rename lands, which would regenerate signals from stale rustdoc JSON and risk an incorrect/false-yellow baseline.

ADR D4 says `track/items/tddd-01-*/domain-types.json` is a historical artifact that will not be retroactively renamed, but D7/T006 and the track spec explicitly require editing `track/items/tddd-01-multilayer-2026-04-12/domain-types.json` as the live catalogue source. That leaves the primary design doc asserting both “do not touch” and “must update” for the same file.

T006 is keyed to deleting `domain-types.json` “entry 30”, but the current catalogue’s only `"Finding"` reference entry is item 4 (`track/items/tddd-01-multilayer-2026-04-12/domain-types.json`). Because the task text names the wrong entry number, an implementer following the plan can target the wrong record or waste time reconciling a phantom mismatch.

ADR D7 weakens the T006 success gate to “blue count increased by at least 1”, even though the same paragraph’s math and the canonical spec/plan require a net `+2` (remove one blue reference, add three blue declarations). A `+1` threshold would let the track pass with one of the replacement declarations still unresolved.

T006 is documented here as regenerating `signals` with `bin/sotp track type-signals --layer domain`, but `type-signals` requires a positional track id. The lower-level task text uses the correct form, so the approved spec is internally inconsistent: an implementer following this in-scope instruction verbatim cannot refresh the catalogue signals.

This planner section says `track/items/tddd-01-*/domain-types.json` is a historical artifact that must not be retroactively renamed, but the ADR and T006 both rely on editing `track/items/tddd-01-multilayer-2026-04-12/domain-types.json` because it is the live catalogue source. That contradiction leaves the research artifact disagreeing with the actual implementation plan about whether the file is frozen or mandatory to change.

The acceptance criterion here uses the wrong blue-count math: removing one blue `Finding` reference entry and adding three blue `declare` entries yields a minimum delta of `+2`, not `+1`. As written, this planner artifact would treat T006 as successful even if one of the three replacement declarations failed to evaluate Blue, which conflicts with ADR D7 and the approved spec.

`knowledge/adr/2026-04-14-0625-finding-taxonomy-cleanup.md` presents D3 as the cascade-rename enumeration, but it is not complete relative to the planner it is supposed to codify: the planner's rename table includes the `libs/domain/src/verify.rs` test call sites and the `libs/domain/src/review_v2/tests.rs` import/assertion updates, while D3 omits them. Because later spec/plan items cite D3 as the source of rename scope, this drift weakens the implementation contract and makes it easier to miss required test-side renames.

The approved spec's T003 in-scope item cites `knowledge/adr/2026-04-14-0625-finding-taxonomy-cleanup.md Consequences Negative (convention drift)`, but that ADR has no such subsection or bullet. The `source-attribution.md` rename is documented in D3, so this extra Blue source is unverifiable provenance and should be corrected or removed before relying on the spec's `blue=38` attribution result.

`knowledge/research/2026-04-14-0625-planner-tddd-04-finding-taxonomy.md` still describes T005 as touching only `codex_reviewer.rs` and `codex_local.rs`, but the same planner's rename table and the live code references also require `libs/infrastructure/src/review_v2/persistence/review_store.rs`, `libs/infrastructure/src/review_v2/persistence/tests.rs`, and `libs/usecase/src/review_v2/tests.rs`. An implementer following the Section 4 task list would stop early and leave stale `review_v2::Finding` consumers behind.

The planner's rollback section still labels T007 as the ADR task even though Section 4 explicitly says ADR-writing T007 was collapsed and the canonical 7-task track now uses T007 for the CI gate. That numbering drift makes the rollback instructions target the wrong task/commit if this plan is executed under failure.

`sotp track type-signals --layer domain` is missing the required `<track-id>` argument here (the CLI entry point takes `track_id: String`, and other track docs use `sotp track type-signals <id> ...`). As written, the T006 signal-regeneration step is not executable, so the ADR and the generated plan/spec summaries are inconsistent about the actual verification command.

The documented collision-check command uses the `cargo make track-baseline-capture` wrapper with the wrong argument shape: `Makefile.toml` defines the wrapper as `cargo make track-baseline-capture -- <track-id> [--force]`. Without that `--`, the track id is not forwarded to `bin/sotp`, so the T006/T007 acceptance check cannot be run as written. The same bad invocation is copied into `verification.md`, `plan.md`, and `metadata.json`.

`metadata.json` still says the collision suppressor in `track/items/tddd-01-multilayer-2026-04-12/domain-types.json` is `entry 30`, and `plan.md` inherits that wording. In the live catalogue it is the 4th `type_definitions` element; `30` is just the current line number. Because T006 is a manual delete-and-replace step against a live file, this stale locator creates cross-document drift and can send an implementer to the wrong JSON entry.

The planner’s Block 6 heading says to `replace entry 30`, but the ADR/spec/T006 description consistently identify the target as the 4th `type_definitions` entry. That leaves the planner disagreeing with the canonical track artifacts about which catalogue item is being removed.

The SSoT task plan uses plain `grep` for its independent verification commands (`grep -rn 'verify::Finding\b'`, `grep -rn '([^a-zA-Z_]|^)Finding::error'`, etc.), but those patterns rely on regex features that GNU `grep` does not interpret the way this plan assumes. In practice they can still match renamed identifiers such as `VerifyFinding`, so a correct implementation can fail the documented gate. Since these commands are the per-task verification steps for T001/T002/T003 (and repeated later), the plan leaves implementers with a broken success criterion.

This spec encodes "`libs/domain` must not add a serde dependency" as a sourced constraint, but `libs/domain/Cargo.toml:14` already declares `serde`. The cited source (`knowledge/conventions/hexagonal-architecture.md`) also does not state the quoted rule. As written, the planning artifacts are enforcing and justifying a factually false architecture constraint, so reviewers cannot tell whether the real rule is "no new serde usage on this type" or something narrower.

The `out_of_scope` exception overstates the historical-ADR carveout: it says `knowledge/adr/2026-04-04-1456-review-system-v2-redesign.md` contains `Finding::error(...)` / `Finding::warning(...)` snippets, but that file has no such occurrences (only plain `Finding` type examples). Because the cited source (`...finding-taxonomy-cleanup.md Consequences Negative`) also only calls out the strict-spec ADR risk, this SSoT item is factually unsupported and can misdirect T007's historical-doc verification to the wrong ADR.

The spec's Option A rationale is inconsistent with the ADR it cites: this item says Option A would 'add a serde dependency' to `libs/domain`, but ADR A1/D6 explicitly says `libs/domain` already depends on `serde` and the real violation is extending serde usage onto the validated domain type. Because `spec.md` is rendered from this JSON, the accepted spec now carries a source-attribution mismatch about why Option A is rejected.

T007's fallback for `verify-arch-docs` failure is not actionable: the spec says that if historical `Finding::error` / `Finding::warning` snippets are linted, adding a note at the top of the ADR will resolve it. If the verifier is checking code blocks, a top-of-file note will not change those snippets, so the plan can still dead-end at the CI gate. The ADR's own Consequences section says the real fallback is to update or wrap the snippets; the task/spec should match that behavior.

The T007 acceptance criterion contradicts the approved scope for historical ADR snippets. `scope.out_of_scope` says the old `Finding::error`/`Finding::warning` pseudo-code in `knowledge/adr/2026-04-12-1200-strict-spec-signal-gate-v2.md` must stay as historical record and, if `verify-arch-docs` complains, the remediation is only to add a note at the top of the ADR. But this acceptance criterion instead authorizes rewriting those snippets (`// historical: ...`) or other code-block handling. That leaves implementers with two incompatible instructions for the same failure mode and can drive an out-of-scope edit of the historical ADR.

`metadata.json`'s S4 summary says a failing `verify-arch-docs` check should be fixed by adding a `Legacy names` note to the ADR, but the ADR, `verification.md`, and the T007 task all say a top-level note is insufficient and that the actual remediation must be wrapping/replacing the offending code blocks. Because `plan.md` is generated from this field, the current plan contains contradictory instructions for the same failure mode and can send the implementer to a no-op fix that still leaves CI red.

The T007 historical-snippet check is calibrated to the wrong baseline. `verification.md` expects `rg 'Finding::error|Finding::warning' knowledge/adr/2026-04-12-1200-strict-spec-signal-gate-v2.md | wc -l` to return `35+`, but the current repository only has 33 matches, so a clean implementation would fail its own manual verification step. The same `35+`/`35` count is repeated in `spec.json`, `spec.md`, `metadata.json`, and `plan.md`, so the track’s verification plan is internally consistent but inconsistent with the repo state it is supposed to verify.

The approved plan is inconsistent about the TDDD command shape: the SSoT goal says `sotp track type-signals --layer domain`, and the ADR similarly shortens `baseline-capture` to `sotp track baseline-capture --layer domain[ --force]`, but both CLI entry points take a required positional `<track-id>`. Other docs in this same track already use the executable form `... tddd-04-finding-taxonomy-cleanup-2026-04-14 --layer domain`, so T006/T007 are not directly runnable from the current approved wording and require the implementer to guess the missing argument.

`§3.B Resolution` says historical ADR and track-artifact references are left as documented history, but the same resolution also requires updating `track/items/tddd-01-multilayer-2026-04-12/domain-types.json`. The new ADR/spec explicitly treat that file as the one exception because it is the live catalogue source, so the parent ADR summary is currently self-contradictory about whether cross-track artifact edits are allowed.

The canonical T007 gate only calls out historical-snippet risk in `knowledge/adr/2026-04-12-1200-strict-spec-signal-gate-v2.md`, but the planner artifact already identified `knowledge/adr/2026-04-04-1456-review-system-v2-redesign.md` as another historical ADR that still contains old `Finding` code. If `verify-arch-docs` starts linting those snippets, the remediation path documented here is incomplete and CI can still fail on an untracked ADR.

S2's section-level summary is internally inconsistent about whether the usecase crate is part of the `review_v2` cascade: the section title/scope says `domain → infra/CLI`, but the same section includes `libs/usecase/src/review_v2/tests.rs` in scope and T005 later requires `cargo test -p usecase`. As written, the section-level verification can be read as allowing implementers to skip the crate whose tests they are changing.

The documented sizing budget for T005 is not consistent across planning artifacts. `spec.json` says T005 is expected to be `~30` lines (and rolls that into the `~370` total), but T005's own task description estimates `~55` lines. Because task-size discipline is a stated constraint, this leaves the plan without a single authoritative budget for that task.

`spec.json` encodes the historical-ADR count command as `rg '\bFinding\b'` with single JSON `\b` escapes, so the generated `spec.md` renders literal backspace characters instead of the intended regex. That leaves the read-only spec view inconsistent with the SSoT and gives implementers a broken verification command for the 8-match check.

T001 can pass verification while still leaving non-historical old names behind. The check here only matches `Finding::error(`/`Finding::warning(`, but current active doc comments in `libs/domain/src/spec.rs:517-521` and `libs/domain/src/tddd/consistency.rs:356-361` use bare `Finding::error`/`Finding::warning` without parentheses. That means a plan-compliant implementation can satisfy this verification and still violate ADR D4's forward-only rename rule (only historical docs may retain the old names).

`spec.json` makes the handling of historical ADR snippets internally inconsistent. In `out_of_scope` it says those old ADR code blocks stay untouched as historical record, but the T007 acceptance criterion says that if `verify-arch-docs` flags them, this track should rewrite the snippets (`// historical: ...` or wrapped blocks). That gives implementers two incompatible paths for the same CI failure and also conflicts with the plan’s claim that T007 is a verification-only task.

T007 is not consistently scoped across the planning artifacts. This task is described here as verification-only with an expected diff of 0, but the same text tells the implementer to wrap or rewrite historical ADR snippets if `verify-arch-docs` fails. That conflicts with the approved spec, which says historical ADR edits are out of scope and should trigger a separate follow-up track, and with ADR 0002 §3.B Resolution saying those historical ADRs are not retroactively edited. As written, an implementer cannot tell whether fixing historical ADR lint belongs in tddd-04 or must be deferred.

Section S2's verification summary says the review_v2 cascade is independently verified with `cargo test -p domain -p infrastructure -p cli`, but the same section includes edits to `libs/usecase/src/review_v2/tests.rs`, and T005 later notes that leaving that file untouched would break `cargo test -p usecase`. This leaves the section-level verification criteria inconsistent with the planned change set and can let S2 be marked complete without exercising the usecase crate it modifies.

`spec.json` marks historical ADR snippet fixes as out of scope and says a failing `verify-arch-docs` run should be deferred to a follow-up track, but its cited source (`knowledge/adr/2026-04-14-0625-finding-taxonomy-cleanup.md` Consequences Negative) says the opposite: if T007 finds those snippets are linted, they must be updated or wrapped. `plan.md`/`verification.md` also still describe in-track remediation. This leaves T007 with two incompatible execution paths (verification-only vs editing historical ADRs), so the implementer cannot tell whether to finish this track or stop and defer.

Section S4 says that if `sotp verify arch-docs` starts failing on historical ADR code blocks, this track should patch those ADRs in place (`code-block wrap` / `// historical:` replacement). That conflicts with the rest of the plan: T007, `spec.json` out-of-scope, `verification.md`, and the ADR all say historical ADR edits are explicitly out of scope for tddd-04 and must be deferred to a follow-up `historical-adr-lint-resolution` track. As written, the execution plan is self-contradictory about whether implementers should stop or edit historical ADRs when that gate fails.

T003 is specified as an import-and-call-site rename only, but several of the listed `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/*.rs` files also contain plain `Finding` type positions that must be renamed for the step to compile after T001, e.g. `latest_track.rs` has `type FileValidator = fn(&Path, &Path) -> Vec<Finding>` plus multiple `Result<..., Vec<Finding>>`/`Vec<Finding>` signatures, `spec_states.rs` returns `Result<Vec<TdddLayerBinding>, Finding>`, and `canonical_modules.rs`/`domain_strings.rs`/`module_size.rs`/`usecase_purity.rs` pass `&mut Vec<Finding>`. Following the current task text literally would leave unresolved `Finding` identifiers even though T003 is supposed to be independently executable.

S4/T007 gives incompatible instructions for the historical-ADR contingency: it first says to stop this track and defer to `historical-adr-lint-resolution`, but the same sentence then says to apply code-block wrapping / `// historical: ...` rewrites if `verify-arch-docs` is scanning code blocks. That conflicts with the ADR/spec position that historical ADR edits are out of scope for tddd-04, so the implementer has no single consistent action if the dry-run fails.

The task split contradicts the claim that each task is an independently verifiable commit: T001/T002 and T004 rename exported domain types while explicitly deferring dependent usecase/infrastructure/CLI updates to later tasks, and D5 forbids compatibility aliases. That leaves the workspace uncompilable after those intermediate commits even though the plan presents them as cleanly sequenced, focused commits. Either fold each public-API rename together with its immediate consumers or relax the 'independently verifiable/compile cleanly per task' claim.

The task model is internally inconsistent. ADR D8 says T001-T007 are each "one focused commit" and independently verifiable, but the approved spec says the no-alias rule forces T001+T002+T003 and T004+T005 to be merged as only 4 atomic commits because T001 alone leaves `usecase` uncompilable and T004 alone leaves downstream consumers broken. An implementer cannot satisfy both the per-task commit ordering and the compile-clean sequencing described for this track.

The verify-side verification gate is stricter than the planned rename scope. `verification.md` requires zero `Finding::error`/`Finding::warning` matches across `libs/ apps/`, explicitly including doc comments, but T001/T003 only describe renaming imports/call sites plus `source-attribution.md`. Current repo state still has standalone doc-comment matches in `libs/domain/src/spec.rs:517-521,1638,1652`, `libs/domain/src/tddd/consistency.rs:356-361`, and `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/spec_states.rs:32-36,186`; unless those sites are added to the task plan, the stated grep gate cannot pass.

The new ADR is internally inconsistent about commit structure: D8 says T001-T007 are grouped into 4 atomic commits, and the generated spec/plan mirror that 4-commit rollout, but Consequences still says the work is '~500 lines across 7 commits.' That contradiction matters because the 4-commit grouping is load-bearing for keeping the workspace compile-clean under the no-alias rule.
